ItacWorkItem.ShortTitle: La Musa Lirica di Ferdinando Di Donno
ItacWorkItem.LongTitle: Divisa in Affetti Platonici | Encomij Amorosi | Amatori Marinareschi | E in Rime Varie.
ItacWorkItem.Illustration: A plate on the title page|snake/human figures; Venus; roses; Cupid; dolphin.
ItacWorkItem.Notes: On p. 100 the author dedicates a poem to the Oziosi Academy. On p. 101 the author mentions the said Academy.
ItacWorkItem.CensorsAgreement: Ego Fr. Io. Ludovicus Secchiarus Raven. Sacra Theologia Lector Ordinis Praedicatorum & Commissarius Sanctissima Inquisitionis Venet. Librum hunc carminum vulgari idiomate inscriptum la Musa Lirica di Ferdinando di Donno omni studio quo valui perlegi & cum nihil quod pias aures offendat continere perspexerim praelo dimisi. Venet. die 12. Aprilis 1620
ItacWorkItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
